description: Add Step Functions state machine with Polly integration task state, wire EventBridge rule to target it, and update ARCHITECTURE.md. Follows strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement minimal CDK code.
steps: Step 1 - Write failing tests in tests/unit/test_cdk_base_stack.py (or a new test file tests/unit/test_step_functions.py). The tests should verify: (a) A Step Functions state machine resource exists (AWS::StepFunctions::StateMachine), (b) The state machine definition contains a Task state that calls Polly (e.g., arn:aws:states:::aws-sdk:polly:synthesizeSpeech or StartSpeechSynthesisTask pattern), (c) The state machine has logging configured (LoggingConfiguration with a CloudWatch Log Group destination), (d) The EventBridge rule targets the Step Functions state machine (the Targets array references a state machine ARN with 'states' in it and has RoleArn for invocation), (e) The IAM execution role has least-privilege Polly permissions (polly:SynthesizeSpeech or polly:StartSpeechSynthesisTask), (f) The EventBridge target passes relevant event data as input (InputTransformer or similar).

Step 2 - Implement the Step Functions state machine in cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py. Use aws_stepfunctions and aws_stepfunctions_tasks L2 constructs. Create a state machine definition: Start -> Polly Task (using CallAwsService or a custom state for Polly synthesizeSpeech/startSpeechSynthesisTask with placeholder parameters like text='placeholder', voice_id='Joanna', output_format='mp3') -> End (Succeed state). Enable CloudWatch Logs on the state machine. Ensure the execution role gets only Polly permissions needed.

Step 3 - Update the EventBridge rule target: replace the placeholder CloudWatch Log Group target with an aws_events_targets.SfnStateMachine target pointing to the new state machine. Configure the target to pass S3 event details (bucket name, object key) as input to the state machine execution using event input transformation. Keep the log group for state machine logging but remove it as the rule target.

Step 4 - Update existing tests if needed. The test_eventbridge_rule_has_target test currently just checks Targets exists - update or add a test to confirm the target is the state machine. The test_log_group_has_one_week_retention test may need adjustment since the log group role changes from rule target to state machine logging destination.
Step 5 - Run NODE_OPTIONS='' pytest tests/ -v to confirm all tests pass.
Step 6 - Run NODE_OPTIONS='' npx cdk synth to verify synthesized template is valid.

Step 7 - Update ARCHITECTURE.md: (a) Change Step Functions status from 'Planned' to 'Implemented' in the status table, (b) Add a note about Polly integration being a skeleton/placeholder, (c) Update the Mermaid diagram to color the SF node green (indicating implemented), (d) Add a short 'Orchestration Layer' section describing the state machine skeleton and how it connects EventBridge to Polly.
acceptance_criteria: An AWS::StepFunctions::StateMachine resource is synthesized in the CloudFormation template
The state machine definition includes at least one Task state invoking Polly (synthesizeSpeech or startSpeechSynthesisTask)
The state machine has CloudWatch Logs logging enabled
The EventBridge rule targets the Step Functions state machine (not the CloudWatch Log Group)
The state machine execution role has least-privilege Polly permissions
Event data (bucket, key) is passed as input to the state machine from EventBridge
All pytest tests pass (both new and existing)
ARCHITECTURE.md is updated with Step Functions status and Mermaid diagram changes
verification: Run NODE_OPTIONS='' pytest tests/ -v and confirm all tests pass
Run NODE_OPTIONS='' npx cdk synth and confirm it succeeds
findings: CDK CallAwsService generates IAM action in camelCase (polly:startSpeechSynthesisTask not polly:StartSpeechSynthesisTask). DefinitionString in CloudFormation template uses Fn::Join intrinsic with AWS::Partition ref, so serialized_json matcher cannot be used for definition assertions - must use find_resources and json.dumps approach instead.
